title: Kerala’s Power department releases short video with tips to keep electricity consumption down
content: State’s Additional Chief Secretary (Power) Puneet Kumar says there is an extraordinary surge in power demand as the summer intensifies, a situation that is putting pressure on both the electricity distribution network and family budgets

title: First stage of counting personnel randomisation completed in Cuddalore
content: Counting of votes recorded in 2,590 polling stations across the district will be taken up on May 4 at four centres

title: ICSE, ISC results 2026 highlights: Overall pass percentage above 99% as girls outperform boys
content: The Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) has announced the results of the Class 10 (ICSE) and Class 12 (ISC) board examinations on April 30, at 11 a.m., officials said.

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ leak case: Madras High Court denies anticipatory bail to one of the accused
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan refuses to grant the relief pursuant to the submissions made by Additional Public Prosecutor K.M.D. Muhilan

title: Supreme Court reserves verdict on Pawan Khera’s plea for pre-arrest bail in Assam case
content: The case is ‘unprecedented’ with Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma himself making statements against the Congress leader, senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi tells the Supreme Court

title: As temperature soar, Telangana Power Minister wants advanced plans to avoid interruptions
content: Usage of air conditioners and coolers at homes and offices, and increase in demand for agricultural purposes pushes demand for electricity

title: Thiruvananthapuram Corporation making all possible interventions to address drinking water scarcity: Mayor Rajesh
content: He says that a lasting solution to the problem can be found only through a major drinking water supply project with the cooperation of the State and Union governments

title: Watch: A look at political defections in India
content: In this week on Talking Politics, we take a look at the long history of defections in India and what it says about Indian politicsl with special guest author and journalist Rasheed Kidwai.

title: Tribal district Parvathipuram-Manyam tops AP SSC 2026 at 96.07% for fourth year
content: The tribal district of Parvathipuram-Manyam topped AP SSC 2026 at 96.07%, retaining first place for the fourth consecutive year on school adoption

title: Bengaluru weather LIVE: Heavy rains and hailstorms throw life out of gear in city
content: 9 killed in city; seven dead in Bowring Hospital wall collapse, two people electrocuted in separate incidents

title: ECI clears ‘Telangana Rakshana Sena’ name for Kavitha’s party, but with conditions
content: The final approval of the party name depends on the objections received to public notice published in newspapers

title: Unused mechanised boat gutted in fire in Rameswaram jetty
content: It is suspected that left over wooden items and some scrap materials beneath the trawler may have caught fire.

title: Gujarat High Court, after attire remarks in court, asks IIT Gandhinagar to ‘show sympathy’ to suspended student
content: Court flags proportionality in disciplinary action, suggests consensus; earlier remarks on attire noted

title: An SIR-marred turnout in West Bengal
content: In absolute terms, the increase in voter turnout in the eastern State was lowest among recent electoral cycles

title: Assembly election 2026 LIVE: TVK's Sengottaiyan rejects exit polls, predicts over 200 seats for party
content: Several exit polls have predicted UDF ahead in Kerala, NDA victory in Assam and Puducherry; DMK alliance is predicted to return in Tamil Nadu

title: Supreme Court directs Allahabad High Court to grant urgent hearing to senior citizen’s plea for pension
content: A Bench comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi asked the High Court to consider the 77-year-old man’s case on a sympathetic basis and give an out-of-turn hearing

title: Ex-Kerala MLA P.V. Anvar vows to ‘shave head’ if defeated in Assembly polls
content: UDF-backed Independent candidate P.V. Anvar says he will ‘shave my head, wear earrings and walk through the streets of Kozhikode’ if he loses the Assembly election in Beypore constituency; challenges LDF’s Mohamed Riyas

title: Supreme Court defers hearing on PIL alleging banking fraud by Anil Ambani-led ADAG to May 8
content: At the outset, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, appearing for the CBI and the Enforcement Directorate, said the two central probe agencies have filed fresh status reports in pursuance of directions issued on March 23 in the case

title: PM Modi wants to delay caste census; details of exercise still awaited: Congress
content: “A full year has passed. The details of how this caste enumeration will be done are still awaited. There has been no dialogue with Opposition parties and state governments, let alone with experts in this subject,” Congress leader Jairam Ramesh said

title: Infosys to add 7,000 IT jobs in Visakhapatnam, gets 20 acres from Andhra Pradesh
content: The IT firm, which began operations in the city from a rented facility in October 2023, has expanded its workforce from 250 to nearly 1,900 in two years

title: ECI recommendation to restrict candidacy from one constituency yet to be implemented: Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say, the recommendation made way back in 2004 is yet to translate into a law

title: Watch: Why is Kerala facing power cuts?
content: Kerala is facing an electricity crunch, and for many residents, that pressure is already being felt through sudden evening power cuts, voltage fluctuations and local outages. The Kerala State Electricity Board, or KSEB, has now authorised power restrictions of up to 30 minutes in selected areas between 6 pm and midnight, the hours when demand usually peaks. So why is this happening now?

title: Aswathaman, key accused in Armstrong murder case, arrested while trying to flee abroad
content: He was released on bail a few days ago, and was apprehended at Bengaluru International Airport while allegedly attempting to escape to Bangkok

title: Sikkim Govt issues advisory on depiction of religious deities in tattoos
content: Sikkim issued a warning against ‘objectionable’ depiction of religious deities after the International Tattoo Convention

title: Kerala rains: Yellow alert for seven districts
content: Alert on Thursday warning of rains in the range of 64.5 mm to 115.5 mm during the next 24 hours has been issued for Thiruvananthapuram, Kollam, Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Idukki and Ernakulam

title: Bengaluru hailstorm damages Advocate General’s office in Karnataka High Court building
content: The annexe building of the Karnataka High Court was impacted from the heavy wind as rain water and hailstones entered the corridors and balconies.

title: Critically endangered Peacock Tarantula in spotlight after Pawan Kalyan post
content: A scientific survey to assess the critically endangered Peacock Tarantula has begun Nagarjunasagar Srisailam Tiger Reserve in Andhra Pradesh, aiming to map its population and habitat

title: BC Dedicated Commission chief visits Visakhapatnam to study reservations in local bodies
content: Retired IAS officer Rajiv Ranjan Mishra met district officials and BC community leaders in Visakhapatnam on Thursday to gather inputs on reservations in local bodies

title: Kerala School Kalolsavam 2026: Full coverage
content: Discover the highlights of vibrant Kerala School Kalolsavam 2026, featuring 14,000 students in 249 events held in Thrissur in this collection of stories on the mega school arts festival

title: Northeast’s maiden Comic Con India stirs latent pop culture
content: The two-day event in Guwahati was the first such celebration beyond the metropolises and “mainland” cities

title: FDI in India may cross $90 billion in 2025-26: DPIIT Secretary
content: He said that during April-February 2025-26, inflows have crossed $88 billion and "hopefully crossing $90 billion" in the full fiscal 2025-26

title: Congress decides not to contest Maharashtra Legislative Council polls; to support MVA candidate
content: The Congress had said it would contest the polls, however, appears to have relented after a call between the party’s Maharashtra in-charge Ramesh Chennithala and Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray

title: Kerala Assembly polls: LDF poised to retain power as exit polls confirm lack of anti-incumbency, claims M.V. Govindan
content: CPI(M) State secretary says while some polling agencies predict a tight race, underlying findings of the surveys, specifically ‘absence of anti-incumbency and popularity of the Chief Minister’, indicate a definitive win for ruling front

title: Mumbai-Pune expressway’s biggest upgrade yet | Missing Link project explained | Pulse Maharashtra
content: The most-anticipated infrastructure project, the Mumbai-Pune Missing Link, reducing the travel time by half an hour, is all set to be inaugurated on May 1.

title: Rajya Sabha polls: ECI directs Odisha CEO to hear BJD’s ‘double ballot’ charge
content: The Election Commission of India's communication, signed by Under Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh, underscores that the grievance of the BJD delegation should be heard on priority

title: Supreme Court asks Allahabad HC panel to take action against bar leaders in Noida court for abstaining from work
content: The apex court noted with concern that the Gautam Buddha Nagar Bar Association has continued to pass resolutions for abstaining from work, despite its judgment explicitly restraining district bar associations from doing so

title: Women quota bill: SP MLAs stage protest against BJP in Uttar Pradesh Assembly premises
content: The protest came ahead of the special one-day session of the Assembly during which the government is expected to move a censure motion against Opposition parties, holding them responsible for the failure to pass the Women’s Reservation Amendment Bill in Lok Sabha

title: Minor's pregnancy: Amend law on pregnancy termination of rape survivors, says Supreme Court
content: The court said in its impassioned observations that this is a case of child rape and the survivor will have a lifelong scar and trauma, if termination is not allowed

title: Bengaluru rains: Homes flooded in Jai Bheem Nagar, residents report repeated losses
content: Residents, many of whom have been living in the area for over 45 years, said they face the same problem every time it rains.

title: Kerala records 99.96% pass in ICSE, 99.97% in ISC exams
content: In Kerala, as many as 7,861 students — 3,795 boys and 4,066 girls — appeared for ICSE examinations from 166 schools, while 3,058 students — 1,551 boys and 1,507 girls — sat for ISC examinations from 85 schools

title: Women BJP MLAs protest against Opposition in Uttar Pradesh Assembly premises
content: The women BJP MLAs assembled in front of Chaudhary Charan Singh's statue in the Assembly premises, holding banners inscribed with the slogan "Insult to Matrushakti (women's power), India will not tolerate it"

title: Two Bangladeshi women arrested for illegal stay in Kerala; 6 others detained in prostitution probe
content: Acting on a tip-off from Kerala Anti-Terrorist Squad, Kazhakoottam police raided a rented house in Karyavattom in State capital on Wednesday night

title: Delhi court grants bail to I-PAC director Vinesh Chandel after no objection from ED
content: Additional Sessions Judge Amit Bansal noted that the Enforcement Directorate (ED) has not opposed the bail plea

title: Kerala Assembly polls: IUML ‘backs’ V.D. Satheesan as Congress-led UDF’s CM pick
content: Indian Union Muslim League State president Panakkad Syed Sadikali Shihab Thangal says UDF will factor in ‘people’s sentiments’ while choosing its CM, noting that exit polls indicate V.D. Satheesan enjoys the highest support

title: Bengaluru rains: More than 100 distressed birds rescued
content: Avian and Reptile Rehabilitation Centre said that more than 100 birds have been rescued by them since April 29.

title: Shivadhar Reddy appointed as Telangana’s State Security Advisor
content: He would advise Telangana govt on issues concerning Police, Law and Order, Internal Security, Crime Control and other aspects

title: Chlorine gas leaks at defunct water purification plant in Pune; 24 persons hospitalised
content: "Following the leak and the subsequent spread of gas, residents in the vicinity complained of breathlessness. Upon receiving the call, fire teams were dispatched. Several citizens were evacuated, and the leaking tank was patched using safety equipment," a fire official said.

title: Bengaluru rains: Three years after fatal flooding, K.R. Circle underpass submerged again
content: According to a police officer, the accumulated water cannot be pumped out as there is no proper outlet, and doing so could flood adjoining roads.

title: AP SSC Result 2026: Class 10 pass percentage at 85.25%, girls outperform boys
content: Andhra Pradesh’s SSC pass percentage rose to 85.25% from 81.14%. Girls outperformed boys; Parvathipuram Manyam topped districts, Alluri Sitharama Raju trailed

title: Digital University Kerala invites applications for PG, PhD programmes
content: The postgraduate entrance test (DUAT) is scheduled for June 1, while the PhD entrance test (DRAT) will be conducted on June 8

title: Supreme Court notice on plea to establish separate ‘revenue judicial’ cadre to decide land disputes
content: The petition drafted by advocate Ashwani Dubey stated that the issue was dealt with by the Allahabad High Court, but its direction has not been implemented in letter and spirit to date

title: Madras High Court orders CBI probe into ₹397-crore transformer procurement during Senthilbalaji’s tenure
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan direct the Directorate of Vigilance and Anti Corruption to handover the papers to CBI within two weeks

title: One of the two tiger cubs rescued in Nilgiris dies
content: Two tiger cubs were taken into captivity, one after the other, after they were believed to have been abandoned by their mother

title: West Bengal polls: VIP treatment for 104-year-old who fought SIR & won
content: A 104-year-old voter, Sk Ibrahim, who has voted since Independence, faced anxiety when his name was initially removed from the electoral roll due to a name discrepancy. Despite missing the vote-from-home option, the East Burdwan administration facilitated his visit to the polling booth. Ibrahim, sharp and lucid, expressed happiness at voting again, questioning the initial adjudication.

title: Riyan Parag slapped with 25% fine and 1 demerit point for vaping during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag has been fined 25% of his match fee and received a demerit point for breaching IPL conduct rules. The incident, captured on live broadcast, involved Parag vaping in the dressing room during a match.

title: Petrol-diesel prices going up? Govt issues clarification, here’s the truth
content: The Press Information Bureau's Fact Check unit has debunked viral claims of a significant hike in petrol and diesel prices, stating no such order has been issued. A senior government official confirmed there's no current proposal to increase retail fuel prices, reassuring consumers amidst global oil market volatility. Fuel prices in major cities remain unchanged.

title: 'No option': Meta launches AI surveillance ahead of job cuts; here's what the CTO said
content: Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth shut down any hopes of opting out of the company's new AI tracking tool, telling employees directly that there's no choice in the matter. The Model Capability Initiative logs keystrokes, mouse movements, and screenshots on work computers to train Meta's AI agents—all while the company prepares to cut 8,000 jobs. Employees are calling it dystopian. Meta is calling it necessary.

title: Weather flips in Delhi-NCR: Hailstorm, rain break heatwave spell
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a sudden weather change on Thursday afternoon with a hailstorm and rain offering relief from heatwave conditions. Dark clouds gathered as the storm began around 3:30 PM, even as the city remained under a yellow alert for heatwaves. This rainfall helped moderate average temperatures, which were lower than last year's April.

title: 'I don't have hope': R Ashwin on Mumbai Indians pulling off 6-match miracle
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's historic IPL 2024 turnaround from 1-7 to playoffs is contrasted with Mumbai Indians' current IPL 2026 struggles. Languishing in ninth place with two wins from eight matches, the Hardik Pandya-led side faces an uphill battle.

title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: Loud DJ music kills 140 chickens in UP's Sultanpur; case filed against operator
content: A poultry farm owner in Sultanpur district claims 140 chickens died after a wedding procession played loud DJ music. The incident occurred on April 25, 2026, near his farm in Dariyapur village. The owner alleges the intense noise caused panic and death among the birds. Police have filed an FIR against the DJ operator.

title: Big relief for Karisma Kapoor’s children: Delhi HC freezes Sunjay Kapur’s assets in India
content: The Delhi High Court has ordered a freeze on the estate of late industrialist Sunjay Kapur. Children of Karisma Kapoor and Sunjay Kapur filed a plea. The court has restrained the creation of third-party rights over assets. It also directed the filing of account statements. Foreign bank accounts and cryptocurrency holdings are also restricted.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection: Ranveer's film races towards 'Baahubali 2' global mark
content: In a remarkable cinematic journey, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is swiftly approaching the monumental box office figures of 'Baahubali 2,' securing its spot as the top-grossing film of the year. After spending six weeks captivating audiences worldwide, it has amassed a staggering Rs 1,782.15 crore, now ranking as the third highest-grossing Indian film of all time. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Oracle makes clarifications on the $300B promise to Sam Altman that's behind layoffs
content: Oracle has staked its entire future on a $300 billion deal with a company that doesn't make money, funded by debt it can't service from operations, built by contractors it doesn't control. Its stock has lost half its value. Thousands of employees have lost their jobs. And its response has been to post on X. This is the story of how Oracle's biggest bet became its biggest liability—and why every reassurance is making it worse.

title: Bengaluru rain havoc: 5,000 books worth Rs 14 lakh destroyed at iconic Church Street bookshop
content: Bengaluru experienced severe disruption as heavy rains flooded streets, metro stations, and even the Vidhana Soudha.  Thousands of books were destroyed at the iconic Bookworm store, with a significant financial loss.  Uprooted trees caused internet outages and traffic snarls, with over 170 trees falling across the city. One woman was injured by a falling tree.

title: Former BJP MLC Manoj Kumar Singh's nephew shot dead in Bihar road rage
content: In a heartbreaking episode in Siwan district, 28-year-old Harsh Kumar Singh lost his life due to a road rage altercation that left his father, Chandan Singh, in critical condition. What started as a minor accident spiraled into chaos when gunfire erupted amidst the escalating confrontation. Authorities are now in pursuit of the assailants with ongoing investigations and raid operations.

title: Chennai horror: Man hacks social media influencer wife to death, then dies by suicide
content: A domestic dispute in Adambakkam tragically ended with a husband allegedly killing his wife, a social media influencer, before taking his own life. The unemployed husband, reportedly facing financial strain, attacked his wife, who was the family's sole earner. Their younger son discovered the grim scene upon returning home. Authorities are investigating the incident.

title: 2 dead as car plunges into 150-foot-deep gorge in Himachal Pradesh
content: Two men died after their car fell into a 150-foot gorge and caught fire on the Bharwain-Hoshiarpur road in Una district. The occupants, returning from Chintpurni temple, were charred to death. In a separate incident, over a dozen Delhi devotees were injured when their mini-van rolled off the road in Bilaspur district.

title: Delhi hit-and-run: Delivery agent falls from flyover after being hit by car, dies
content: A food delivery agent tragically died after falling from the Dabri flyover in Delhi. His scooter was reportedly struck by a car, whose driver fled the scene. The victim, identified as Rajkumar, sustained severe injuries and was declared dead at a hospital. Police are investigating the incident and searching for the absconding driver.

title: Iran’s ‘secret weapon’ that could give US-Israel forces a 'heart attack'
content: Iran's Navy commander warns of a new, feared weapon soon to be unveiled, amid escalating US-Israel tensions. He claims Iranian forces conducted missile strikes on a US aircraft carrier and launched numerous reprisal attacks, while tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz. The commander also accused US forces of piracy and hostage-taking.

title: No lungi, no vote? CAPF faces backlash in Bengal as elderly voters stopped over attire
content: Central forces personnel at a polling booth in Gaighata allegedly prevented elderly men from voting in their lungis, citing dress code violations. This led to protests from villagers and Trinamool members, who accused the forces of harassment. The restriction was later withdrawn after the unrest.

title: 'Sonia Gandhi takes our decisions': Kharge on speculations over change of CM in Karnataka
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge downplayed the Karnataka chief ministerial tussle between Siddaramaiah and DK Shivakumar, stating the party will resolve the "confusion soon." This comes amid speculation fueled by a reported power-sharing agreement and a statement from Home Minister G Parameshwara suggesting Kharge could be a suitable CM.

title: Exit poll results 2026 for Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry: Explained in 5 charts
content: Exit polls suggest a mixed verdict across states as West Bengal anticipates a hung assembly, potentially ending Mamata Banerjee's tenure. Tamil Nadu might see actor Vijay's TVK emerge as a significant force, challenging the established duopoly. Assam favors a BJP hat-trick, while Kerala's Left government faces a potential defeat. Puducherry's NDA is expected to retain power.

title: Vande Bharat Express: 1st direct train between Jammu & Kashmir flagged off; check details
content: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on Thursday flagged off the 20-coach Vande Bharat Express connecting Jammu and Srinagar, marking the first direct rail link between the two capitals of Jammu and Kashmir.

title: 'Gas leak' closes London's Farringdon station, few passengers 'feeling unwell'
content: Farringdon station, a vital London transport hub, was evacuated this morning due to a suspected gas leak, causing widespread disruption. Emergency services responded after passengers reported feeling unwell. The incident led to the suspension of the Elizabeth line and significant delays on multiple Underground and Thameslink services, with the station closed until at least  noon.

title: 'We didn't fall for nuclear bluff': Rajnath Singh slams Pakistan, explains why Op Sindoor was halted
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh stated India voluntarily halted Operation Sindoor on its own terms, asserting readiness for a prolonged conflict with Pakistan, which he labeled the "epicentre of International Terrorism." He emphasised India's "zero tolerance" for terrorism and its sponsors, highlighting the nation's robust military capabilities and resolve against nuclear threats.

title: China holds the world’s largest strategic oil reserves; US, Japan follow; where does India stand?
content: Based on current estimates, the largest strategic crude reserves at the end of December 2025 were held by China, which significantly expanded its holdings during 2025.

title: IPL 2026 playoff scenarios: What MI need to do and why time is running out
content: Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 playoff hopes are in dire straits after a six-wicket loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad. Despite a stellar 123* from Ryan Rickelton, MI's bowling woes persist, leaving them with a daunting task of winning nearly all remaining matches. Their poor net run rate further complicates qualification, demanding dominant victories. With 2 wins in 8 games (4 points) and a NRR of -0.784, they are far from the pack.

title: US-Iran war cost: $25bn or just a low-ball figure? What the Pentagon told Congress
content: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion, though sources suggest the true figure could reach $50 billion due to unincluded repair costs. Officials defended the ongoing conflict, calling it an "existential fight" and dismissing concerns of it becoming a "quagmire."

title: 1 dead, 4 hurt as ‘baraatis’ lose cool over cooler air in Azamgarh
content: In the heart of Azamgarh, a wedding that promised love and joy devolved into a nightmare as a quarrel about a desert cooler's placement ignited a violent confrontation. Sadly, the incident claimed the life of Amir Chandra Sahani and left four other attendees in critical condition.

title: 'Imagine the pain, humiliation': SC urges Centre to allow abortion beyond 20 weeks for rape survivors
content: The Supreme Court urged the Centre to consider amending laws to allow rape survivors to terminate pregnancies beyond 20 weeks, emphasizing no time limit in such cases. The bench highlighted the lifelong trauma for a child survivor and the need for the decision to rest with the survivor and her parents, with AIIMS offering counsel.

title: No-look six off Bumrah, 300 strike-rate: Salil Arora steals the spotlight in Wankhede run-fest
content: Young Salil Arora's audacious 10-ball 30, featuring a stunning no-look six off Jasprit Bumrah, stole the show in a high-scoring IPL 2026 match.  Despite a star-studded Sunrisers Hyderabad lineup, Arora's fearless finishing, including an unbeaten 30, sealed a dominant chase, repaying the franchise's faith in his potential. Despite modest returns initially, SRH continued to back Arora in the lower order. That faith was repaid last night.

title: Navy, DRDO demonstrate salvo launch capability of indigenous naval anti-ship missile
content: India's Navy and DRDO successfully tested the Naval Anti-Ship Missile–Short Range from a Sea King helicopter off Odisha. The salvo firing demonstrated the missile's ability to strike a ship's vulnerable waterline, with two missiles launched in quick succession. This advanced 'fire-and-forget' weapon, featuring a 100 kg warhead and 55 km range, promises enhanced naval defense capabilities.

title: Shardul Thakur: Impact sub without impact as Mumbai Indians’ call raises questions
content: The Mumbai Indians left fans scratching their heads by employing Shardul Thakur as an Impact Sub while he never got the chance to bowl a single ball. This curious tactic came after a dismal display against Sunrisers Hyderabad, where Thakur's experience could have potentially turned the tides. On a day when every MI bowler went at an economy of over 10, it was baffling to see the bowler with over 100 IPL wickets not getting a bowl.

title: 'What the hell is wrong?': Chaos at Mumbai airport due to inordinate SpiceJet flight cancellations
content: SpiceJet flights from Mumbai experienced significant disruptions, with multiple flights to Bangalore, Delhi, and Ahmedabad facing lengthy delays and cancellations. Passengers endured sleepless nights at the airport due to these issues. This follows a trend of poor on-time performance for SpiceJet, which recorded the lowest OTP among Indian carriers in March.

title: 'Strait of Trump': US President shares new map naming Hormuz after himself
content: US President Donald Trump shared a modified map renaming the Strait of Hormuz as the "Strait of Trump," following claims that a naval blockade against Iran could last for months. This development occurs as Iran rejects US claims about economic damage and criticizes the blockade strategy, with its parliament speaker calling the advice "junk."

title: 'Fellow MLAs will remain outside': Punjab CM to meet President Murmu over seven AAP RS MPs joining BJP
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann will meet President Murmu on May 5 to "safeguard the mandate of the people" following the defection of seven AAP Rajya Sabha MPs to the BJP. Mann, accompanied by party MLAs, will press for the "recall" of the MPs, though legal provisions for such a recall are absent.

title: Samrat Choudhary cabinet removes Sanjay Gandhi's name from Patna zoo, dairy institute
content: In a significant leap towards enhancing transportation infrastructure, Bihar's cabinet has sanctioned four pivotal road projects. Among these are two exciting scenic routes along the Ganga, reminiscent of the celebrated JP Ganga Path in Patna, designed to enhance accessibility.

title: Why is stock market down today? Sensex crashes over 1,100 points
content: The sell-off was widespread, extending beyond large-cap stocks to the broader market. The Nifty Smallcap 100 index was down 0.5%, while the Nifty Midcap 100 index fell more than 1%.

title: 'They are on a similar time-table': Did Trump just mix-up Ukraine with Iran? Watch
content: Former US President Donald Trump linked the Ukraine conflict with US operations against Iran. He suggested both situations might follow a similar timeline. Trump also stated Ukraine was militarily defeated. He discussed a conversation with Russian President Vladimir Putin regarding a ceasefire in Ukraine and assistance with Iran. Putin reportedly offered help on Iran's uranium issue.

title: Revenge killing in Manesar: Sarpanch’s son guns down father’s killer out on parole
content: A parolee, Sundar Fauji, accused in a 2018 murder, was shot dead in broad daylight in Kasan village. Police suspect a revenge killing, with the assailant reportedly being the son of the man Fauji was accused of murdering. The suspect fled the scene, and authorities are actively searching for him.

title: Iran's uranium, Russia's offer, US response: What did Putin and Trump discuss?
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin offered to help manage Iran's enriched uranium stockpile during a call with US President Donald Trump. Trump, however, prioritized Putin's involvement in ending the Ukraine war, stating his main concern is the conflict in Ukraine. The US president also indicated a blockade in the Strait of Hormuz would continue to pressure Iran over its nuclear program.

title: 'Jasprit Bumrah hasn't been up to mark, but he has still been the No.1 bowler '
content: Despite Jasprit Bumrah's struggles in IPL 2026 with only two wickets in eight matches, Mumbai Indians' batting coach Kieron Pollard backed the star pacer. Pollard emphasized that even a bowling genius is allowed an off phase and urged for leniency, believing Bumrah will bounce back stronger.

title: SIR divides families across Kolkata: Chosen few celebrate vote as lakhs mourn deletion
content: Kolkata's special electoral roll revision left many families fractured on poll day. While some voted, lakhs were disenfranchised as their names were deleted.  Instances like Mariam Zaki, whose father and grandfather couldn't vote, and former MP Tarun Mandal highlight the widespread disappointment and confusion caused by the process, leaving many questioning the fairness of the deletions.

title: Supreme Court asks petitioner to turn in first-gen copy of leaked audio to forensic lab
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . content: A member of the TOI UP bureau team, Shalabh brings extensive field reporting experience. While covering various government departments, he focuses on issues with the potential to significantly impact the public, both positively and negatively. Read More

title: 'Why no affidavit?' SC raps Centre over airfare fluctuations, demands reply by May 8
content: the TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of the Times of India . the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce .

title: PM Modi To Unveil Projects Worth Rs 8,000 Crore In Telangana On May 10
content: Among the key projects lined up are new facilities at AIIMS Bibinagar and the expansion of MMTS Phase 2, along with national highway and railway works.

title: Majority Claims, Defection Fears, And A Possible Punjab Floor Test
content: Seven AAP MPs, including Raghav Chadha, have crossed over to the BJP. After their joining, the speculation has intensified concerns about internal cracks within the ruling party.

title: 'Can Print Everything Except Money': ISRO Acquires Russian 3D Printer

title: Clean Energy Investments More Consequential Than Many Realise: Jeffrey Sachs

title: Rupee At Record Low: Oil-Dollar-War Trap Outweighing RBI's Countermoves?

title: Trump Has 3 "Military Options" Against Iran Amid Stalemate. What They Are

title: Why Iran's Oil Industry Is Being Squeezed By US Blockade

title: Man Carrying Sister's Skeleton To Bank Sparks Uproar In Odisha Assembly

title: Hezbollah Adopts A New Weapon Against Israel: Fiber-Optic Drones
content: Fiber-optic drones have a thin cable that connects an operator directly to the drone, making it impossible to electronically jam.

title: Outrage In Gujarat Village After Dalits Denied Entry To Temple Feast

title: When AI Marks Wheat As Chana, Tomato, Madhya Pradesh Farmers Pay The Price
content: In Madhya Pradesh government's digital records, some wheat fields are shown as gram, mustard, tomatoes, chillies or vacant land.

title: Trump Shares Map Renaming Hormuz As 'Strait Of Trump' Amid Naval Blockade
content: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow but vital sea route through which about one-fifth of global oil shipments typically pass.

title: Rajnath Singh Compares AI To 'Bhasmasur', Says "Can't Just See Rosy Picture"
content: Comparing AI's use case to the mythological demon 'Bhasmasur', Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said that the technology can affect several sectors if it falls into the wrong hands.
